subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: Transition Metals Introduction The d-block of the periodic table consists of elements from groups 3-12 where d orbitals are progressively filled. They are known as transition metals.
text_html:
Transition Metals Introduction
The d-block of the periodic table consists of elements from groups 3-12 where d orbitals are progressively filled. They are known as transition metals.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: Inner Transition Metals Elements filling 4f and 5f orbitals make up the f-block, often referred to as inner transition metals. They are placed separately at the bottom of the periodic table.
text_html:
Inner Transition Metals
Elements filling 4f and 5f orbitals make up the f-block, often referred to as inner transition metals. They are placed separately at the bottom of the periodic table.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: Series of Transition Metals 3d series: Scandium (Sc) to Zinc (Zn) 4d series: Yttrium (Y) to Cadmium (Cd) 5d series: Lanthanum (La) including Hafnium (Hf) to Mercury (Hg) 6d series: Actinium (Ac) and elements from Rutherfordium (Rf) to Copernicium (Cn)
text_html:
Series of Transition Metals
  • 3d series: Scandium (Sc) to Zinc (Zn)
  • 4d series: Yttrium (Y) to Cadmium (Cd)
  • 5d series: Lanthanum (La) including Hafnium (Hf) to Mercury (Hg)
  • 6d series: Actinium (Ac) and elements from Rutherfordium (Rf) to Copernicium (Cn)

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: Series of Inner Transition Metals 4f series: Cerium (Ce) to Lutetium (Lu) - Lanthanoids 5f series: Thorium (Th) to Lawrencium (Lr) - Actinoids
text_html:
Series of Inner Transition Metals
  • 4f series: Cerium (Ce) to Lutetium (Lu) - Lanthanoids
  • 5f series: Thorium (Th) to Lawrencium (Lr) - Actinoids

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: IUPAC Definition of Transition Metals Transition metals have incomplete d subshells either in their neutral atom state or in their ions, according to IUPAC.
text_html:
IUPAC Definition of Transition Metals
Transition metals have incomplete d subshells either in their neutral atom state or in their ions, according to IUPAC.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: Excluding Group 12 from Transition Metals Zinc, cadmium, and mercury have full d 10 configuration and are not typically regarded as transition metals, but their chemistry is studied along with other transition elements.
text_html:
Excluding Group 12 from Transition Metals
Zinc, cadmium, and mercury have full d10 configuration and are not typically regarded as transition metals, but their chemistry is studied along with other transition elements.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: Characteristics of Transition Elements The partially filled d or f orbitals make transition elements unique from non-transition elements and influence their distinct properties.
text_html:
Characteristics of Transition Elements
The partially filled d or f orbitals make transition elements unique from non-transition elements and influence their distinct properties.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: Applications of Transition Metals Precious metals like silver, gold, and platinum as well as industrially vital metals such as iron, copper, and titanium are classified as transition metals.
text_html:
Applications of Transition Metals
Precious metals like silver, gold, and platinum as well as industrially vital metals such as iron, copper, and titanium are classified as transition metals.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: Focus of Study in Transition Elements This study unit covers electronic configurations, occurrence, and general characteristics with emphasis on trends of first-row (3d) transition elements.
text_html:
Focus of Study in Transition Elements
This study unit covers electronic configurations, occurrence, and general characteristics with emphasis on trends of first-row (3d) transition elements.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Position in The Periodic Table
text: Inner Transition Elements Study Focus The unit also explores general aspects like electronic configurations, oxidation states, and chemical reactivity of inner transition metals.
text_html:
Inner Transition Elements Study Focus
The unit also explores general aspects like electronic configurations, oxidation states, and chemical reactivity of inner transition metals.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Electronic Configuration of Transition Elements The general configuration for transition elements: ​ {tex}(n-1) d^{1-10} ns^{1-2}{/tex} is common, with exceptions such as Pd, which is ​ {tex}4d^{10} 5s^0{/tex} .
text_html:
Electronic Configuration of Transition Elements
The general configuration for transition elements: ​{tex}(n-1) d^{1-10} ns^{1-2}{/tex} is common, with exceptions such as Pd, which is ​{tex}4d^{10} 5s^0{/tex}.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Stability Factors The preference for half or fully filled d-orbitals in stability results in exceptions for Cr ​(3d^5 4s^1) and Cu ​(3d^{10} 4s^1). The energy difference between d and s orbitals is minimal.
text_html:
Stability Factors
The preference for half or fully filled d-orbitals in stability results in exceptions for Cr ​(3d^5 4s^1) and Cu ​(3d^{10} 4s^1). The energy difference between d and s orbitals is minimal.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Standard Electronic Configuration Patterns Transition elements often display a ​(n-1) {tex}d{/tex} and ns orbital configuration, with energies that dictate their filled positions.
text_html:
Standard Electronic Configuration Patterns
Transition elements often display a ​(n-1){tex}d{/tex} and ns orbital configuration, with energies that dictate their filled positions.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Unique Transition Metal Configuration Transition metals like Zn, Cd, Hg, and Cn have completely filled d-orbitals in the ground state (​ {tex}(n-1) d^{10} ns^2{/tex} ). They are typically not considered true transition elements.
text_html:
Unique Transition Metal Configuration
Transition metals like Zn, Cd, Hg, and Cn have completely filled d-orbitals in the ground state (​{tex}(n-1) d^{10} ns^2{/tex}). They are typically not considered true transition elements.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Metallic Characteristics Transition elements show variety in oxidation states. They form color ions and complex compounds. Their ​(d^{1-9}) configurations contribute to magnetic and electronic properties.
text_html:
Metallic Characteristics

  • Transition elements show variety in oxidation states.
  • They form color ions and complex compounds.
  • Their ​(d^{1-9}) configurations contribute to magnetic and electronic properties.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Behavior in Chemical Reactions Transition metals and their compounds often act as catalysts and exhibit paramagnetism due to unpaired d-electrons.
text_html:
Behavior in Chemical Reactions
Transition metals and their compounds often act as catalysts and exhibit paramagnetism due to unpaired d-electrons.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Properties Across Transition Series Horizontal similarities in a transition series can often be more significant than those in non-transition elements.
text_html:
Properties Across Transition Series
Horizontal similarities in a transition series can often be more significant than those in non-transition elements.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Detailed Examples from Series Use **Table 4.1** as a reference to study detailed examples of electronic configurations across different rows: 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 4th series.
text_html:
Detailed Examples from Series
Use **Table 4.1** as a reference to study detailed examples of electronic configurations across different rows: 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 4th series.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Contributions of ​(d\) Orbitals The ​(d\) orbitals' proximity to the atomic boundary affects interactions with nearby atoms or molecules, influencing bonding and coordination.
text_html:
Contributions of ​(d\) Orbitals
The ​(d\) orbitals' proximity to the atomic boundary affects interactions with nearby atoms or molecules, influencing bonding and coordination.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Electronic Configurations of The d-Block Elements
text: Study Focus Areas General transition properties, trends across rows, and individual group similarities. Main focus on 3d transition row characteristics and definitions.
text_html:
Study Focus Areas
  • General transition properties, trends across rows, and individual group similarities.
  • Main focus on 3d transition row characteristics and definitions.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Basic Properties of Transition Elements Nearly all transition elements exhibit high tensile strength, ductility, malleability, and high thermal and electrical conductivity, except Zn, Cd, Hg, and Mn.
text_html:
Basic Properties of Transition Elements
Nearly all transition elements exhibit high tensile strength, ductility, malleability, and high thermal and electrical conductivity, except Zn, Cd, Hg, and Mn.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Lattice Structures Transition metals typically have metallic structures such as hcp, bcc, and ccp. Scandium to Copper have varied lattice structures with exceptions like Mn and Tc.
text_html:
Lattice Structures
  • Transition metals typically have metallic structures such as hcp, bcc, and ccp.
  • Scandium to Copper have varied lattice structures with exceptions like Mn and Tc.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Melting and Boiling Points Transition metals generally have high melting and boiling points due to electrons involved in interatomic metallic bonding.
text_html:
Melting and Boiling Points
Transition metals generally have high melting and boiling points due to electrons involved in interatomic metallic bonding.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Enthalpy of Atomisation Higher enthalpies of atomisation indicate stronger bonding. It notably increases across the transition metal series, contributing to noble behavior.
text_html:
Enthalpy of Atomisation
Higher enthalpies of atomisation indicate stronger bonding. It notably increases across the transition metal series, contributing to noble behavior.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Atomic and Ionic Sizes There is a progressive decrease in atomic and ionic radii within a series due to ineffective d electron shielding.
text_html:
Atomic and Ionic Sizes
There is a progressive decrease in atomic and ionic radii within a series due to ineffective d electron shielding.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Ionisation Enthalpies Trend Ionisation enthalpies generally increase from left to right due to increased nuclear charge. The increase is less steep than in non-transition elements.
text_html:
Ionisation Enthalpies Trend
  • Ionisation enthalpies generally increase from left to right due to increased nuclear charge.
  • The increase is less steep than in non-transition elements.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Oxidation States Variety Middle-series elements like Manganese exhibit a range of oxidation states (+2 to +7). The shifting states arise from incomplete d orbital filling.
text_html:
Oxidation States Variety
  • Middle-series elements like Manganese exhibit a range of oxidation states (+2 to +7).
  • The shifting states arise from incomplete d orbital filling.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Standard Electrode Potentials The electrode potential values indicate trends in chemical reactivity and stability; lower values show stability for certain oxidation states.
text_html:
Standard Electrode Potentials
The electrode potential values indicate trends in chemical reactivity and stability; lower values show stability for certain oxidation states.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Chemical Reactivity Some transition metals are reactive with mineral acids, while nobler metals remain inert or require oxidising acids for reaction.
text_html:
Chemical Reactivity
Some transition metals are reactive with mineral acids, while nobler metals remain inert or require oxidising acids for reaction.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: General Properties of The Transition Elements (d-Block)
text: Catalytic and Alloy Properties Transition elements serve as catalysts due to multiple oxidation states. They form alloys important in the industrial domain.
text_html:
Catalytic and Alloy Properties
  • Transition elements serve as catalysts due to multiple oxidation states.
  • They form alloys important in the industrial domain.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: Formation of Metal Oxides Metal oxides are formed by metals reacting with oxygen at elevated temperatures. Examples of such oxides are MO oxides, which are ionic, except for scandium. The oxidation state of these oxides corresponds to their group number in the periodic table.
text_html:
Formation of Metal Oxides
  • Metal oxides are formed by metals reacting with oxygen at elevated temperatures.
  • Examples of such oxides are MO oxides, which are ionic, except for scandium.
  • The oxidation state of these oxides corresponds to their group number in the periodic table.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: Oxidation States Beyond Group 7 There are no higher oxides of iron beyond Fe2O3. V5+ is stabilized as VO2 + , and Ti4+ as TiO2 + .
text_html:
Oxidation States Beyond Group 7
  • There are no higher oxides of iron beyond Fe2O3.
  • V5+ is stabilized as VO2+, and Ti4+ as TiO2+.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: Decreasing Ionic Character As the metal oxidation number rises, the ionic character diminishes. Higher metal oxides like Mn2O7, which appear covalent, demonstrate this property.
text_html:
Decreasing Ionic Character
  • As the metal oxidation number rises, the ionic character diminishes.
  • Higher metal oxides like Mn2O7, which appear covalent, demonstrate this property.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: Acidic Character of Higher Oxides Higher oxides show primarily acidic nature. Mn2O7 forms HMnO4, while CrO3 forms H2CrO4 and H2Cr2O7. V2O5 is amphoteric but mainly acidic.
text_html:
Acidic Character of Higher Oxides
  • Higher oxides show primarily acidic nature.
  • Mn2O7 forms HMnO4, while CrO3 forms H2CrO4 and H2Cr2O7.
  • V2O5 is amphoteric but mainly acidic.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: Chromium Compound Preparation Potassium dichromate, K2Cr2O7, is significant in the leather industry and for making azo dyes. It is made by reacting sodium chromate with a solution of potassium chloride.
text_html:
Chromium Compound Preparation
  • Potassium dichromate, K2Cr2O7, is significant in the leather industry and for making azo dyes.
  • It is made by reacting sodium chromate with a solution of potassium chloride.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: Interconversion in Chromates and Dichromates Chromates and dichromates are interconvertible in aqueous solution, influenced by pH. Both have the same oxidation state of chromium.
text_html:
Interconversion in Chromates and Dichromates
  • Chromates and dichromates are interconvertible in aqueous solution, influenced by pH.
  • Both have the same oxidation state of chromium.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: Structure of Monoatomic Ions Chromate ion, CrO4 2- , is tetrahedral in shape. The dichromate ion, Cr2O7 2- , consists of two tetrahedra.
text_html:
Structure of Monoatomic Ions
  • Chromate ion, CrO42-, is tetrahedral in shape.
  • The dichromate ion, Cr2O72-, consists of two tetrahedra.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: Preparation of Potassium Permanganate Potassium permanganate is prepared via fusion of MnO2 with an alkali metal and an oxidizing agent. It has physical properties like intense color and weak paramagnetism.
text_html:
Preparation of Potassium Permanganate
  • Potassium permanganate is prepared via fusion of MnO2 with an alkali metal and an oxidizing agent.
  • It has physical properties like intense color and weak paramagnetism.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: The Chemistry of Lanthanoids Lanthanoids include the fourteen elements following lanthanum. They have only one stable oxidation state and slight size and nuclear charge variations.
text_html:
The Chemistry of Lanthanoids
  • Lanthanoids include the fourteen elements following lanthanum.
  • They have only one stable oxidation state and slight size and nuclear charge variations.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
text: The Actinoid Series The actinoids follow actinium in the periodic table. They exhibit a complex chemistry with various oxidation states and radioactivity-related challenges.
text_html:
The Actinoid Series
  • The actinoids follow actinium in the periodic table.
  • They exhibit a complex chemistry with various oxidation states and radioactivity-related challenges.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: General Characteristics of Lanthanoids All lanthanoids tarnish quickly in air and are soft metals with increasing hardness from lanthanum to lutetium. Their melting points range from 1000 to 1623 K, and they have a typical metallic structure, making them good heat and electricity conductors.
text_html:
General Characteristics of Lanthanoids
All lanthanoids tarnish quickly in air and are soft metals with increasing hardness from lanthanum to lutetium. Their melting points range from 1000 to 1623 K, and they have a typical metallic structure, making them good heat and electricity conductors.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: Electronic Configurations Lanthanoids typically have a {tex} 6 s^2 {/tex} configuration with variable occupancy of the {tex} 4 f {/tex} subshell. Their commonly stable oxidation state is tripositive, denoted as {tex} 4f^n {/tex} , where {tex} n {/tex} varies from 1 to 14 with an increasing atomic number.
text_html:
Electronic Configurations
Lanthanoids typically have a {tex} 6 s^2 {/tex} configuration with variable occupancy of the {tex} 4 f {/tex} subshell. Their commonly stable oxidation state is tripositive, denoted as {tex} 4f^n {/tex}, where {tex} n {/tex} varies from 1 to 14 with an increasing atomic number.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: Lanthanoid Contraction The lanthanoid contraction refers to the steady decrease in atomic and ionic radii from lanthanum to lutetium. This occurs due to imperfect electron shielding within the $$ 4 f $$ subshell, contributing significantly to the chemical behavior of these and subsequent transition metals.
text_html:
Lanthanoid Contraction
The lanthanoid contraction refers to the steady decrease in atomic and ionic radii from lanthanum to lutetium. This occurs due to imperfect electron shielding within the $$ 4 f $$ subshell, contributing significantly to the chemical behavior of these and subsequent transition metals.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: Oxidation States Lanthanoids predominantly form La(II) and Ln(III) compounds but can also form +2 and +4 ions due to the extra stability of certain {tex} f {/tex} subshell configurations. For example, Ce(IV) has a noble gas configuration and is a strong oxidant despite reverting to +3.
text_html:
Oxidation States
Lanthanoids predominantly form La(II) and Ln(III) compounds but can also form +2 and +4 ions due to the extra stability of certain {tex} f {/tex} subshell configurations. For example, Ce(IV) has a noble gas configuration and is a strong oxidant despite reverting to +3.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: Chemical Reactivity These metals show increased reactivity with decreasing atomic number, behaving like calcium initially and transitioning towards aluminum-like behavior. They can form complex compounds with hydrogen, carbon, and halogens, displaying rich chemistry.
text_html:
Chemical Reactivity
These metals show increased reactivity with decreasing atomic number, behaving like calcium initially and transitioning towards aluminum-like behavior. They can form complex compounds with hydrogen, carbon, and halogens, displaying rich chemistry.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: Uses of Lanthanoids A major application of lanthanoids is in producing alloy steels like mischmetall, which is used to manufacture bullets, shell casings, and lighter flints. Their oxides are also employed as catalysts and phosphors in televisions and fluorescent surfaces.
text_html:
Uses of Lanthanoids
A major application of lanthanoids is in producing alloy steels like mischmetall, which is used to manufacture bullets, shell casings, and lighter flints. Their oxides are also employed as catalysts and phosphors in televisions and fluorescent surfaces.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: Coloured Ions Trivalent lanthanoid ions exhibit colors in solid state and aqueous solutions due to {tex} f {/tex} electrons. Despite their narrow absorption bands, these ions exhibit paramagnetism excluding {tex} f^0 {/tex} (La3+ and Ce4+) and {tex} f^{14} {/tex} (Yb2+ and Lu3+) states.
text_html:
Coloured Ions
Trivalent lanthanoid ions exhibit colors in solid state and aqueous solutions due to {tex} f {/tex} electrons. Despite their narrow absorption bands, these ions exhibit paramagnetism excluding {tex} f^0 {/tex} (La3+ and Ce4+) and {tex} f^{14} {/tex} (Yb2+ and Lu3+) states.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: Ionic and Metallic Properties Lanthanoids form ionic bonds typically depicted as {tex} Ln^{3+} {/tex} ions and combine with minerals to produce metals like in mischmetall. Their metallic form shows conductive properties and reacts with dilute acids to release hydrogen.
text_html:
Ionic and Metallic Properties
Lanthanoids form ionic bonds typically depicted as {tex} Ln^{3+} {/tex} ions and combine with minerals to produce metals like in mischmetall. Their metallic form shows conductive properties and reacts with dilute acids to release hydrogen.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: Ionisation Energies Lanthanoids have a first ionisation enthalpy of around 600 kJ mol^-1, similar to calcium. The variation in third ionisation enthalpy, influenced by half-filled orbitals, indicates enhanced stability in such configurations.
text_html:
Ionisation Energies
Lanthanoids have a first ionisation enthalpy of around 600 kJ mol^-1, similar to calcium. The variation in third ionisation enthalpy, influenced by half-filled orbitals, indicates enhanced stability in such configurations.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Lanthanoids
text: Table 4.9: Electronic Configurations and Radii Table 4.9 details the atomic number, name, symbol, electronic configurations, and radii of each lanthanoid. This aids in understanding the unique characteristics and variability in metallic properties across this series.
text_html:
Table 4.9: Electronic Configurations and Radii
Table 4.9 details the atomic number, name, symbol, electronic configurations, and radii of each lanthanoid. This aids in understanding the unique characteristics and variability in metallic properties across this series.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: Actinoid Series The actinoids encompass the fourteen elements ranging from Thorium (Th, Z=90) to Lawrencium (Lr, Z=103). They are all radioactive with varying half-lives.
text_html:
Actinoid Series
The actinoids encompass the fourteen elements ranging from Thorium (Th, Z=90) to Lawrencium (Lr, Z=103). They are all radioactive with varying half-lives.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: Electronic Configurations Actinoids have a common electronic configuration of 7s 2 , with occupancy of the 5f and 6d subshells. The 5f electrons start filling from Protactinium (Pa, Z=91).
text_html:
Electronic Configurations
Actinoids have a common electronic configuration of 7s2, with occupancy of the 5f and 6d subshells. The 5f electrons start filling from Protactinium (Pa, Z=91).

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: Ionic Sizes There is a gradual decrease in size within the actinoid series, known as the actinoid contraction, due to poor shielding by 5f electrons.
text_html:
Ionic Sizes
There is a gradual decrease in size within the actinoid series, known as the actinoid contraction, due to poor shielding by 5f electrons.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: Oxidation States Actinoids generally exhibit a +3 oxidation state, with higher oxidation states observed in elements at the start of the series, such as Th, Pa, U, and Np.
text_html:
Oxidation States
Actinoids generally exhibit a +3 oxidation state, with higher oxidation states observed in elements at the start of the series, such as Th, Pa, U, and Np.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: General Characteristics Actinoid metals are mainly silvery with a variation in structure due to differences in metallic radii, more significant than in lanthanoids.
text_html:
General Characteristics
Actinoid metals are mainly silvery with a variation in structure due to differences in metallic radii, more significant than in lanthanoids.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: Reactivity Actinoids are highly reactive, combining readily with non-metals and attacked by hydrochloric acid. Their reactivity decreases with the formation of protective oxide layers.
text_html:
Reactivity
Actinoids are highly reactive, combining readily with non-metals and attacked by hydrochloric acid. Their reactivity decreases with the formation of protective oxide layers.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: Magnetic Properties The magnetic susceptibility of actinoids is complex but parallels that of lanthanoids. However, actinoids typically display weaker magnetic susceptibility.
text_html:
Magnetic Properties
The magnetic susceptibility of actinoids is complex but parallels that of lanthanoids. However, actinoids typically display weaker magnetic susceptibility.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: Ionization Enthalpy Early actinoids have lower ionization enthalpies than early lanthanoids as 5f electrons are more shielded and available for bonding.
text_html:
Ionization Enthalpy
Early actinoids have lower ionization enthalpies than early lanthanoids as 5f electrons are more shielded and available for bonding.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: Comparison with Lanthanoids Actinoids begin to resemble lanthanoids in characteristics during the latter half of the series, like gradual variation in properties and contraction effects.
text_html:
Comparison with Lanthanoids
Actinoids begin to resemble lanthanoids in characteristics during the latter half of the series, like gradual variation in properties and contraction effects.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: The Actinoids
text: Lanthanoid vs. Actinoid Contraction Both series experience contraction, affecting succeeding elements' sizes. However, the lanthanoid contraction is more studied due to the obscurity surrounding post-actinoid chemistry.
text_html:
Lanthanoid vs. Actinoid Contraction
Both series experience contraction, affecting succeeding elements' sizes. However, the lanthanoid contraction is more studied due to the obscurity surrounding post-actinoid chemistry.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Iron and Steel Production Iron and steels are crucial construction materials. Their production involves reducing iron oxides, eliminating impurities, and adding carbon and alloying metals such as Cr, Mn, and Ni.
text_html:
Iron and Steel Production
Iron and steels are crucial construction materials. Their production involves reducing iron oxides, eliminating impurities, and adding carbon and alloying metals such as Cr, Mn, and Ni.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Specialized Compounds Specialized compounds are manufactured for specific purposes: • **TiO2** is used in the pigment industry. • **MnO2** plays a role in dry battery cells. • **Zn and Ni/Cd** are essential for the battery industry.
text_html:
Specialized Compounds
Specialized compounds are manufactured for specific purposes:
• **TiO2** is used in the pigment industry.
• **MnO2** plays a role in dry battery cells.
• **Zn and Ni/Cd** are essential for the battery industry.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Coinage Metals The Group 11 elements, also known as the coinage metals, include: • Ag and Au, mostly used for collections. • UK 'copper' coins made of copper-coated steel. • UK 'silver' coins as Cu/Ni alloy.
text_html:
Coinage Metals
The Group 11 elements, also known as the coinage metals, include:
• Ag and Au, mostly used for collections.
• UK 'copper' coins made of copper-coated steel.
• UK 'silver' coins as Cu/Ni alloy.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Catalytic Roles Many metals and compounds are vital catalysts in the chemical industry: • **V2O5** catalyzes SO2 oxidation in sulfuric acid production. • **TiCl4 with Al(CH3)3** forms Ziegler catalysts for polyethylene production. • Iron catalysts facilitate the Haber process for ammonia synthesis.
text_html:
Catalytic Roles
Many metals and compounds are vital catalysts in the chemical industry:
• **V2O5** catalyzes SO2 oxidation in sulfuric acid production.
• **TiCl4 with Al(CH3)3** forms Ziegler catalysts for polyethylene production.
• Iron catalysts facilitate the Haber process for ammonia synthesis.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Photographic and Chemical Applications • AgBr is significant due to its light sensitivity in photography. • Nickel is instrumental in hydrogenating fats. • The Wacker process uses PdCl2 for ethyne oxidation to ethanal.
text_html:
Photographic and Chemical Applications
• AgBr is significant due to its light sensitivity in photography.
• Nickel is instrumental in hydrogenating fats.
• The Wacker process uses PdCl2 for ethyne oxidation to ethanal.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Block Structures in Periodic Table • **d-block:** Groups 3-12, where inner d orbitals fill progressively. • **f-block:** Located outside at the table bottom, features progressive filling of 4f and 5f orbitals.
text_html:
Block Structures in Periodic Table
• **d-block:** Groups 3-12, where inner d orbitals fill progressively.
• **f-block:** Located outside at the table bottom, features progressive filling of 4f and 5f orbitals.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Metallic Properties of Transition Elements • High tensile strength, ductility, malleability, and conductivity. • High melting/boiling points due to strong interatomic bonding. • Ionisation enthalpies do not steeply rise as atomic number increases.
text_html:
Metallic Properties of Transition Elements
• High tensile strength, ductility, malleability, and conductivity.
• High melting/boiling points due to strong interatomic bonding.
• Ionisation enthalpies do not steeply rise as atomic number increases.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Chemical Behavior and Compound Formation • Transition metals form compounds with O, N, S, and halogens. • Reactivity varies among metals; example: Copper is less reactive. • Notable oxides include the formation of potassium dichromate and permanganate.
text_html:
Chemical Behavior and Compound Formation
• Transition metals form compounds with O, N, S, and halogens.
• Reactivity varies among metals; example: Copper is less reactive.
• Notable oxides include the formation of potassium dichromate and permanganate.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Inner Transition Elements Lanthanoids and actinoids form the f-block: • Lanthanoids: Show gradual size decrease, with +3 oxidation state being common. • Actinoids: Exhibit complex chemistry with multiple oxidation states, many are radioactive.
text_html:
Inner Transition Elements
Lanthanoids and actinoids form the f-block:
• Lanthanoids: Show gradual size decrease, with +3 oxidation state being common.
• Actinoids: Exhibit complex chemistry with multiple oxidation states, many are radioactive.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements
topic: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
text: Applications of d- and f-block Elements • Used in steel varieties, catalysts, and complexes. • Play roles in organic synthesis processes and other technological applications.
text_html:
Applications of d- and f-block Elements
• Used in steel varieties, catalysts, and complexes.
• Play roles in organic synthesis processes and other technological applications.
